steps.title: Calculate the slope from the two known points
steps.content:
The company earns a profit of \$220 when selling 8 items and a profit of \$320 when selling 10 items. Using the slope formula with points $(8, 220)$ and $(10, 320)$:
$$m = \frac{320 - 220}{10 - 8} = \frac{100}{2} = 50$$
The slope is 50, meaning profit increases by \$50 per item sold.

steps.title: Use slope and a point to find the linear equation
steps.content:
With slope $m = 50$ and the point $(8, 220)$, use the form $f(x) = mx + b$:
$$220 = 50(8) + b$$
$$220 = 400 + b$$
$$b = -180$$
Therefore $f(x) = 50x - 180$. That matches choice D — D is correct.
